example: Every article warning that Big Tech has become 'a company with the powers of a state' is retelling the East India Company story — the corporation that ended up ruling 200 million people.
nuance: chartered company（特许公司）承接第9课的 charter 概念——从城市特许状到公司特许状是同一制度工具的演化。荷兰同名公司 the Dutch East India Company (VOC) 常称史上第一家股份公司。“垄断”= monopoly；“国中之国”= a state within a state。
etymology: charter 见前——特许公司：主权外包的雏形。

synonyms.sentence: A monopoly with an army stops being a company.
Company rule taxed a subcontinent for shareholders.

zh: 物种与商品的洲际交流
def_zh: 殖民贸易将美洲的马铃薯、玉米、番茄、烟草、可可及亚洲茶叶等运回欧洲，欧洲的葡萄酒、啤酒、毛纺织品销往殖民地；欧洲文化亦传入殖民地，对当地社会发展影响深远。（教材描述的这一双向流动，英语史学称“哥伦布大交换”。）
def_en: The two-way traffic colonisation opened: potatoes, maize, tomatoes, tobacco and cocoa flowing to Europe, European goods, people, culture — and, historians add, diseases — flowing back; the process Alfred Crosby named the Columbian Exchange.

sources.chapter: 第16课 早期殖民掠夺

collocations: Old World and New World crops
example: Ireland's famine, Sichuan's chillies and Italy's tomatoes are all footnotes to the same event: the Columbian Exchange rewired every cuisine and population curve on earth.
nuance: the Columbian Exchange 是英语史学标准术语（Crosby 1972），中文教材描述其内容而未命名——典型的“概念在场、术语缺位”案例，正是本知识库要补的那类映射。相关词：cash crop（经济作物）、staple crop（主粮）。
etymology: exchange 源自拉丁 ex + cambiare（交换）——哥伦布“大交换”：物种的全球对调。

synonyms.sentence: Biological globalisation began with pigs and smallpox ashore.
The transfer moved crops one way and empires the other.

def_zh: 英格兰1215年颁布的文件：维护教俗贵族特权，规定未经协商国王无权征税，逐渐确立“王权有限”“王在法下”的基本原则；13世纪末英格兰确立上下两院的议会制度，征税权归议会。
def_en: The charter of 1215 that, in defending baronial and clerical privilege, fixed the principles that royal power is limited and the king is under the law — no taxation without counsel; by century's end England had a two-house Parliament holding the power of the purse.

sources.chapter: 第17课 君主立宪制的英国

example: Campaigners call every foundational rights document 'a Magna Carta for the digital age' — eight centuries on, the brand still means putting rulers under rules.
nuance: 拉丁名 Magna Carta（大宪章）直接用于英语，习惯不加 the。“王在法下”= the king is under the law（布拉克顿名言）；“征税权在议会”即 the power of the purse——美国政治报道高频词。上院/下院 = the House of Lords / the House of Commons。
etymology: charta（文书）+ magna（大）——“大宪章”即“大文书”，朴素得史诗。

synonyms.sentence: Due process began as barons' self-defence.
Limited government is a king on a leash of parchment.

def_zh: 詹姆士一世狂热推崇的理论：王权为上帝所赐，国王是上帝派到人间的统治者，神圣不可侵犯——与英国“王权有限”的法律政治传统严重冲突，其不经议会强行征税使议会与王权对立。
def_en: The doctrine James I preached — kingship as God's gift, the monarch His deputy on earth, answerable to no one — which collided head-on with England's tradition of limited, law-bound kingship the moment he taxed without Parliament.

sources.chapter: 第17课 君主立宪制的英国

example: Founders with golden shares govern by divine right of kings, minus the theology: unaccountable power justified by special anointment.
nuance: 固定术语 the divine right of kings。对比中国“天命”= the Mandate of Heaven——关键差异：天命可因失德而转移（革命的合法性来源），divine right 理论上不可转移，正因此才需要革命去打破它。詹姆士一世的绰号“基督教世界最聪明的大傻瓜”= the wisest fool in Christendom。
etymology: divine 源自拉丁 divus（神的）——“神授”即“神之权利”。

synonyms.sentence: Absolutism preached from pulpits it appointed.
Legitimacy is power explaining itself.

def_zh: 1628年议会以《权利请愿书》重申未经议会同意不得征税等原则，查理一世得拨款后解散议会；1640年议会重开、抨击王权专断，揭开革命序幕；1642年查理一世讨伐议会挑起内战，议会军获胜，1649年查理一世上断头台，英国宣布为共和国——但权力落入克伦威尔手中，革命废除了君主制，却未终结个人专权。
def_en: Parliament's Petition of Right (1628) restated the old limits; Charles I pocketed the subsidy and dissolved the house. Recalled in 1640, Parliament's assault on royal absolutism opened the revolution; Charles made war on it in 1642, lost, and in 1649 was beheaded, England declared a commonwealth — whose power promptly settled on Cromwell: monarchy abolished, one-man rule not.

sources.chapter: 第17课 君主立宪制的英国

example: 1649 remains the precedent every constitutional crisis gestures at: even kings can be tried, convicted and executed by the institutions they defy.
nuance: 命名即史学立场：英语通称 the English Civil War（内战），马克思主义传统称 the English (Bourgeois) Revolution（资产阶级革命），辉格史学亦有 the Puritan Revolution。“断头台”此处实为斩首台 the scaffold（法国才是 guillotine）；“弑君”= regicide。
etymology: regicide 源自拉丁 rex（王）+ caedere（杀）——弑君，1649年的技术名词。

synonyms.sentence: The civil war tried the king and the constitution together.
The commonwealth was a republic embarrassed by its general.

def_zh: 内战中的议会军统帅，战功卓著、声望极高，内战后掌握共和国最高权力，受尊“护国主”而独揽大权、凌驾议会；1658年去世后军官争权、政局动荡，新贵族与资产阶级遂以承认议会权力为条件复辟斯图亚特王朝。
def_en: Parliament's victorious general, who as Lord Protector towered over the very institution he had fought for; his death in 1658 loosed a scramble among the officers, and the propertied classes restored the Stuarts — on condition that Parliament's rights be honoured.

sources.chapter: 第17课 君主立宪制的英国

example: Revolutions keep minting Cromwells: the general who wins the republic's war and then discovers the republic cannot be trusted without him.
nuance: “护国主”= Lord Protector，其政权称 the Protectorate。“复辟”= the Restoration（斯图亚特复辟为专名）。成语 warts and all（不加美化）出自克伦威尔要求画师如实画他。爱尔兰对其记忆极负面——英语语境提及需知其争议性。
etymology: protectorate 源自 protector（保护者）——护国公体制：共和的独裁化名。

synonyms.sentence: Military rule signs itself 'temporary'.
The puritan closed the theatres and opened the question.

def_zh: 詹姆士二世在英国恢复天主教与专制、倒行逆施；1688年议会废黜之，迎其女玛丽与女婿威廉入主英国——不流血的政变，史称“光荣革命”。
def_en: When James II set about restoring Catholicism and absolutism, Parliament in 1688 simply replaced him — inviting his daughter Mary and her husband William to the throne in a bloodless coup remembered as 'glorious' precisely because no one died for it.

sources.chapter: 第17课 君主立宪制的英国

example: Boardroom transitions get the same adjective: a 'glorious revolution' is any regime change accomplished by lawyers and letters rather than blood.
nuance: “政变”一般译 coup (d'état)，但英语史学从不称1688为 coup——命名本身是辉格史观的胜利。经济学中1688年是制度经济学的明星案例（North & Weingast：credible commitment 可信承诺——议会主权使英国国债利率骤降），Economist 讨论制度与增长常引。
etymology: bloodless（无血的）+ revolution——“光荣”在于未流血，命名即宣传。

synonyms.sentence: Regime change by invitation letter.
The settlement traded a crown for a contract.

def_zh: 1689年英国议会通过，重申英国人“自古就有的权利”：议会定期召开、议员言论自由、征税权属议会、国民可自由请愿；国王不经议会不得废止法律、不得征税、平时不得维持常备军。威廉夫妇接受后，议会权力日益超过国王，君主立宪制逐渐形成。
def_en: Parliament's 1689 settlement of 'ancient rights': regular parliaments, free speech within them, taxation and standing armies only by parliamentary consent, no royal suspension of law. William and Mary took the crown on these terms — and the balance of power tipped permanently toward Parliament.

sources.chapter: 第17课 君主立宪制的英国

example: Westminster's core doctrine — parliamentary sovereignty — dates from this bargain: the crown wears the crown, but the Commons signs the cheques.
nuance: 区分三个同名文献：英国 Bill of Rights (1689)、美国 the Bill of Rights（宪法前十修正案，1791）、各国泛称的 bill of rights（权利法案）。“自古就有的权利”修辞值得注意——英国宪政变革惯以“恢复旧制”包装创新（invented tradition）。
etymology: sovereignty 见前；parliament 源自法语 parler（说话）——议会即“说话的地方”。

synonyms.sentence: The settlement made the purse mightier than the sceptre.
A rights declaration is memory armed against relapse.

def_zh: 又称议会君主制：议会行使国家最高权力，君主是国家权力的象征。英国经长期探索选择保留王权形式的资产阶级代议政体；1689年《权利法案》、1701年《王位继承法》以立法限制王权，18世纪内阁制度逐步确立，代议制度进一步完善。
def_en: Monarchy under law: parliament holds supreme power while the crown symbolises the state. Britain built it piecemeal — the Bill of Rights, the Act of Settlement (1701) fixing succession by statute, then the 18th-century rise of cabinet government completing representative rule beneath a ceremonial throne.

sources.chapter: 第17课 君主立宪制的英国

example: The British formula — the monarch reigns but does not rule — proved so exportable that half the world's stablest democracies keep a crowned figurehead on precisely these terms.
nuance: “代议制”= representative government/democracy；“内阁制”= cabinet government（衍生 parliamentary system 议会制，与 presidential system 总统制对举——比较政治的基本分野）。注意英国宪法是不成文宪法 an unwritten/uncodified constitution。与日本幕府的“天皇虚位”形式相似而原理相反：一个权力在议会，一个在将军。
etymology: constitution 见前 + -ism——立宪主义：权力的合同化。

synonyms.sentence: A limited monarchy reigns by consent and ribbon-cutting.
In a parliamentary system the executive is parliament's tenant.

def_zh: 英国视北美13殖民地为原料产地与商品市场；1765年后一系列新税法激化矛盾，1773年东印度公司垄断茶叶贸易引发波士顿倾茶事件与高压法令。1775年来克星顿枪声开战；大陆会议整编大陆军、华盛顿任总司令。1777年萨拉托加大捷为转折点，法国参战；1781年约克镇英军投降，1783年英国承认美国独立——既是民族解放战争，也是资产阶级革命。
def_en: Taxed after 1765 to pay Britain's war debts and squeezed by the East India Company's tea monopoly — answered by the Boston Tea Party — the thirteen colonies went to war at Lexington in 1775, with Washington commanding the Continental Army. Saratoga (1777) turned France from sympathiser to ally; Yorktown (1781) forced surrender; in 1783 Britain conceded independence — a war of national liberation that was also, the textbook notes, a bourgeois revolution.

sources.chapter: 第18课 美国的独立

collocations: the shot heard round the world
example: The Tea Party lends its name to every anti-tax revolt in American politics — the founding tantrum, endlessly re-enacted.
nuance: 革命的口号 no taxation without representation（无代表不纳税）直通《大宪章》传统。“一分钟人”= minutemen（今为导弹名）。来克星顿枪声英语称 the shot heard round the world（爱默生诗句）。美英各自称呼：美称 the Revolutionary War，英称 the American War of Independence。
etymology: independence = in-（不）+ dependere（悬挂于）——独立即“不再悬挂于”。

synonyms.sentence: Secession from empire, federation among themselves.
The slogan priced consent into tax law.

def_zh: 弗吉尼亚种植园主之子，早年任军队指挥官；独立战争中团结各州、坚持对英作战，赢得独立；1787年主持制宪会议；1789年当选美国第一任总统，首都以其名命名。
def_en: Virginia planter's son and wartime commander who held the states together to victory, chaired the Constitutional Convention, and became the first president — then, as memorably, gave the office up.

sources.chapter: 第18课 美国的独立

collocations: first in war, first in peace
example: Washington's greatest act was an abdication: by walking away after two terms he set the precedent that the office outranks the man — the norm every strongman since has tested.
nuance: “开国元勋”= the founding fathers（美国政治话语核心词，现多用 founders/framers 求性别中性；framers 特指制宪者）。注意教材未提的关键点在英语叙事中极重：主动交权（peaceful transfer of power）是华盛顿神话的核心。
etymology: precedent 见前——华盛顿的辞职即最伟大的“先例”。

synonyms.sentence: The commander-in-chief resigned his sword to found the habit.
The farewell address warned against the parties it predated.

def_zh: 1776年7月4日大陆会议通过、杰斐逊起草：宣布人人生而平等，享有生命权、自由权和追求幸福的权利；政府正当权力得自被统治者的同意，人民有权改变或废除损害这些目的的政府。第一个以国家名义表述资产阶级政治要求的纲领性文献，称“第一个人权宣言”——但未废除奴隶制，天赋人权事实上不包括黑人和印第安人。
def_en: Jefferson's charter of 1776: all men are created equal, endowed with unalienable rights to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness; governments derive their just powers from the consent of the governed, and may be altered or abolished when they betray those ends. The first state paper to found a nation on rights — while leaving slavery intact, its self-evident truths withheld in fact from Black and Native Americans.

sources.chapter: 第18课 美国的独立

collocations: life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness
example: American political argument is Declaration exegesis: every movement from abolition to civil rights has demanded the country honour the cheque Jefferson wrote.
nuance: 关键句英文原文值得整句记忆：We hold these truths to be self-evident, that all men are created equal…“天赋人权”对应 natural/unalienable rights（源自洛克 natural rights 传统）；“政府正当权力得自被统治者同意”= the consent of the governed——社会契约论的宪章化。
etymology: alien（他者）→ alienable（可让渡）→ un-（不可）——“不可让渡的权利”。

synonyms.sentence: Natural rights claim a title older than kings.
Consent of the governed is legitimacy's new landlord.

def_zh: 独立后各州各行其是、中央软弱；1787年费城制宪会议经争论妥协制定宪法，依分权制衡原则设计联邦制共和国：行政、立法、司法三权分立，总统、国会、最高法院相互制衡；联邦与地方分权；总统议员由选举产生。世界第一部资产阶级成文宪法——但允许奴隶制存在，不承认妇女、黑人和印第安人享有平等政治权利。
def_en: With the young union drifting apart under a feeble centre, the Philadelphia Convention of 1787 hammered out — through argument and compromise — a federal republic on the principle of checks and balances: powers separated among president, Congress and Supreme Court, sovereignty shared between federal and state governments, offices filled by election. The first written national constitution of its kind — which also countenanced slavery and excluded women, Black people and Native Americans from equal citizenship.

sources.chapter: 第18课 美国的独立

example: Gridlock is not a bug: the Constitution was engineered so that ambition would counteract ambition, and Washington's paralysis is the machine working as designed.
nuance: 三权分立 = the separation of powers；制衡 = checks and balances；联邦制 = federalism——这三个词是美国政治报道的骨架。“制宪会议”= the Constitutional Convention；“妥协”著名者如 the three-fifths compromise（五分之三妥协，即奴隶制条款）。孟德斯鸠的分权理论在此落地。
etymology: federalism 源自拉丁 foedus（盟约）——联邦即“盟约之治”。

synonyms.sentence: Separation of powers sets ambition to guard ambition.
An amendment is the founders admitting sequels.

def_zh: 18世纪以法国为中心、波及欧洲的反对旧制度的思想文化运动：思想家著书立说宣传自由、平等、民主，反对专制，提倡以理性之光驱散愚昧黑暗（法语“启蒙”即“光明”）。伏尔泰为旗手、推崇英国政制；孟德斯鸠提倡分权制衡；卢梭主张公民最大限度直接参与国家管理。为法国大革命作了重要理论准备。
def_en: The 18th-century movement, centred on France, that turned reason's light on the old order: liberty, equality and democracy argued in print against despotism and superstition. Voltaire, its standard-bearer, admired England's constitution; Montesquieu theorised the separation of powers; Rousseau wanted citizens governing themselves directly — together writing the French Revolution's intellectual script.

sources.chapter: 第19课 法国大革命和拿破仑帝国

example: Both sides of the culture war claim 1750: defenders of 'Enlightenment values' and critics of its blind spots are fighting over which half of the inheritance counts.
nuance: the Enlightenment 定冠词大写为专名；泛用 enlightenment 又是佛教“开悟”译词——语境定音。“启蒙思想家”法语借词 the philosophes（专指此群体）。康德的运动纲领 Sapere aude（敢于求知）常被引用。“理性”= reason，衍生 the Age of Reason。
etymology: en-（使）+ light（光）——启蒙即“点亮”；德 Aufklärung、法 Lumières 同象。

synonyms.sentence: The Age of Reason footnoted its way to revolutions.
The philosophes wrote for salons and posterity.

en: the ancien régime and the Estates-General
def_zh: 18世纪法国封建制度日益腐朽，进步思想家称之为“旧制度”。社会分三等级：教士（第一等级）掌精神权力、免税；贵族（第二等级）亦免税；余下多数人为第三等级。三级会议按等级分别开会、各有一票，仅国王征税时不定期召集——1789年前已175年未开。
def_en: France's decaying old order — the ancien régime, as its critics christened it: clergy (First Estate) and nobility (Second) privileged and untaxed, everyone else the Third Estate. The Estates-General voted by order, one vote each, and met only when the king wanted money — not once in the 175 years before 1789.

sources.chapter: 第19课 法国大革命和拿破仑帝国

example: Commentators reach for 'ancien régime' whenever an establishment looks ripe for toppling — privileged, broke and sure the old rules still hold.
nuance: ancien régime 以法语原词进入英语，泛指任何“注定被推翻的旧秩序”。等级 = estate；衍生词妙用：the fourth estate（第四等级=新闻界，Economist 自称所属）。西哀士名句可配读：What is the Third Estate? Everything.
etymology: 法语“旧制度”直借——大革命命名其敌，遂成通用名。

synonyms.sentence: The estates voted by order until the third refused.
Privilege is law with a private entrance — privata lex.

def_zh: 旧制度制约经济、援美独立致债台高筑，1789年路易十六被迫召开三级会议议征税；第三等级自行成立国民议会（后改制宪议会），国王暗中调兵，7月14日巴黎民众攻占巴士底狱，革命爆发。制宪议会废除贵族特权与封建地租，1791年制宪确立新制度框架。1792年废君主制建法兰西第一共和国，1793年路易十六以叛国罪上断头台；雅各宾派罗伯斯庇尔救国委员会平叛退敌、将革命推向高潮，但打击面过宽，罗伯斯庇尔亦死于政变。大革命摧毁法国君主统治，传播自由民主思想，具有世界性影响。
def_en: Bankrupted partly by bankrolling America, Louis XVI summoned the Estates-General in 1789; the Third Estate declared itself the nation's assembly, Paris stormed the Bastille on 14 July, and the revolution swept away noble privilege and feudal dues. A republic followed in 1792, the king's head in 1793; Robespierre's Jacobin Committee of Public Safety saved the revolution by terror and then devoured him too. The monarchy was destroyed — and liberty, equality and fraternity became a world export.

sources.chapter: 第19课 法国大革命和拿破仑帝国

example: Bastille Day is the template for revolutionary anniversaries everywhere, and 'storming the Bastille' the stock phrase for any crowd breaking into any citadel of privilege.
nuance: 断头台此处才是 the guillotine。“恐怖统治”= the Reign of Terror（雅各宾专政的英语定名）；“革命吞噬自己的儿女”（Vergniaud）已成英语成语。国歌《马赛曲》= la Marseillaise。左右翼（left/right wing）的政治语义即诞生于制宪议会的座次——这是 Economist 每页都在用的词的出生证。
etymology: terror 源自拉丁 terrere（使恐惧）——“恐怖统治”使普通词成专名。

synonyms.sentence: The revolutionary calendar restarted time itself.
The guillotine democratised execution.

en: the Declaration of the Rights of Man
en_alt: the Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen
def_zh: 1789年8月制宪议会通过，宣告人权、法治、自由、分权、平等和保护私有财产等基本原则：法律是公共意志的表现，全国公民都有权参与制定，法律面前人人平等。1791年宪法将其置于正文之前，但把公民分为“积极公民”与“消极公民”，与宣言原则并不完全一致。
def_en: The National Assembly's August 1789 charter: rights, the rule of law, liberty, separation of powers, equality and property; law as the expression of the general will, made by all and equal for all. The 1791 constitution enshrined it — then divided Frenchmen into 'active' and 'passive' citizens, an early lesson in the gap between declarations and constitutions.

sources.chapter: 第19课 法国大革命和拿破仑帝国

example: The UN's Universal Declaration of 1948 is this text gone global — same title logic, same tension between proclaiming rights and enforcing them.
nuance: 全名 the Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen——“人与公民”的双重身份是启蒙政治学的精髓。“公共意志”= the general will（卢梭术语 volonté générale 的英译）。与美国《独立宣言》对读：一个论证脱离，一个规定原则。
etymology: right 古英语 riht（直、正）——权利与“正直”同源，中译“权利”则取“权衡之利”。

synonyms.sentence: Universalism proclaimed rights it took centuries to deliver.
The citizen replaced the subject overnight, on paper.

def_zh: 科西嘉贵族出身的炮兵军官，1799年政变组建新政府、建立高效国家机器，重财政兴工商；1804年经公民投票称帝，建法兰西第一帝国。主持制定《拿破仑法典》（1804），体现自由平等与私有财产神圣不可侵犯原则，至今施行、为多国民法蓝本。大军横扫欧洲、废除各地封建特权，也压榨掠夺各国人民；1812年远征俄国大败，1815年滑铁卢战败，帝国覆灭，终老圣赫勒拿岛。
def_en: The Corsican artillery officer who seized power in 1799, rebuilt the French state, and crowned himself emperor by plebiscite in 1804. His Civil Code — liberty, equality before the law, the inviolability of property — still governs France and patterned civil law worldwide; his armies exported the revolution's reforms at bayonet-point while plundering the continent, until Russia's snows and Waterloo ended the empire and exiled him to St Helena.

sources.chapter: 第19课 法国大革命和拿破仑帝国

example: 'Meeting your Waterloo' is English's standard idiom for the final, career-ending defeat — Napoleon's contribution to the language of failure being larger than most men's to success.
nuance: meet one's Waterloo（遭遇最终惨败）为高频成语；Napoleonic 形容雄心勃勃的集权风格；a Napoleon complex 指小个子的好胜心理（俗）。《拿破仑法典》= the Napoleonic Code / the French Civil Code——承接罗马法→《查士丁尼法典》→大陆法系的完整线索。“公民投票”= plebiscite/referendum（plebiscite 常带被操纵的暗示，正合拿破仑用法）。
etymology: code 见前——《拿破仑法典》使 code 几乎专指民法典。

synonyms.sentence: An emperor by plebiscite is a new grammar of crown.
Everyone meets a Waterloo; his had cavalry.

def_zh: 17世纪末君主立宪制建立后英国政局稳定、市场扩大，棉纺织品需求刺激技术革新：1733年凯伊发明飞梭，1765年哈格里夫斯发明珍妮纺纱机（改进后同纺80根纱线），各部门纷纷机器化——生产领域的革命性变化。19世纪中期英国成为世界上第一个工业国家，人类进入“蒸汽时代”；法美德等国先后跟进。
def_en: With politics settled after 1689 and markets swelling, Britain's cotton boom set off a chain of invention — Kay's flying shuttle (1733), Hargreaves's spinning jenny (1765, soon spinning eighty threads at once) — that mechanised one trade after another. By mid-19th century Britain was the world's first industrial nation and humanity had entered the age of steam, with France, America and Germany racing to follow.

sources.chapter: 第20课 第一次工业革命

example: Every 'fourth industrial revolution' keynote is numbered off this one: the original remains the benchmark for what happens when technology, capital and institutions finally align.
nuance: 教材的因果链（立宪→稳定→市场→革新）正是制度经济学的标准叙事——links 到光荣革命。“珍妮机”= the spinning jenny；“世界工场”= the workshop of the world（英国的维多利亚时代称号，今常移用于中国）。工业革命分期：First（蒸汽）、Second（电气）、Third（信息）、Fourth（AI，达沃斯营销词）。
etymology: industry 源自拉丁 industria（勤勉）——“工业”本义勤奋，革命后才指机器。

synonyms.sentence: Mechanisation moved the muscle to coal.
The factory age clocked humanity in.

def_zh: 早期蒸汽机仅用于矿井抽水；18世纪中期瓦特系列改进，1785年瓦特蒸汽机投入使用提供便利动力，化工、冶金、采矿相继采用，19世纪30年代成为主要动力来源——生产领域意义重大的飞跃，使工厂摆脱水力、可设于远离河流处，规模更大。
def_en: Steam began as a mine pump; Watt's improvements (in use from 1785) turned it into general-purpose power, adopted across chemicals, metallurgy and mining until, by the 1830s, steam was the prime mover — freeing factories from the riverbank and letting them grow wherever coal could reach.

sources.chapter: 第20课 第一次工业革命

example: Economists call electricity and AI 'general-purpose technologies' by analogy with steam: inventions whose real product is every other industry's productivity.
nuance: horsepower（马力）即瓦特为推销蒸汽机发明的营销单位；功率单位 watt 以他命名——一个人名同时进入物理学与日常语。习语：run out of steam（后劲不足）、let off steam（发泄）、full steam ahead（全速前进）——蒸汽时代的语言化石。
etymology: steam 古英语 stēam（蒸汽）；engine 拉丁 ingenium（天赋、巧智）——引擎即“巧思之物”。

synonyms.sentence: The prime mover freed factories from the riverbank.
Horsepower priced the horse out of work.

def_zh: 工厂有集中的生产场所、生产纪律和管理制度，与家庭生产不同。初期成年男工不愿进厂，工厂只能雇用妇女和贫民救济院的童工，纺纱工人常日工作十四五个小时；进入19世纪，传统手工工场逐渐被大工厂替代，现代工厂制度最终确立。
def_en: Concentrated premises, work discipline and management — everything cottage production was not. Early mills, shunned by adult men, ran on women and pauper children working fourteen-hour days; by the 19th century the factory had displaced the manufactory for good, and with it came the industrial way of life.

sources.chapter: 第20课 第一次工业革命

example: Debates about warehouse algorithms are factory-discipline debates in new clothes: who owns the worker's minute has been the question since the first mill bell rang.
nuance: factory（机器工厂）自此才取得现代义——与殖民时代的 factory（商馆）区分。布莱克诗句 dark satanic mills（黑暗的撒旦磨坊）是英语中工厂制度阴暗面的标准意象。“童工”= child labour；“血汗工厂”= sweatshop。
etymology: factory 源自拉丁 facere（制作）——先指商馆（factor 代理人之所），后指工厂。

synonyms.sentence: The mill bell replaced the sun.
The shop floor is where policy meets sweat.

def_zh: 1825年斯蒂芬森设计的蒸汽机车载450名乘客正式试车，标志铁路时代开始；1830年后英国掀起铁路投资狂潮，至1851年建成约1万千米铁路网——提供更快捷、廉价、便利的交通，使生产与市场联系更加密切。
def_en: Stephenson's locomotive hauled 450 passengers in 1825 and opened the railway age; the investment mania after 1830 gave Britain 10,000 kilometres of track by 1851 — cheap, fast carriage that fused production and market into one economy.

sources.chapter: 第20课 第一次工业革命

example: The 1840s railway mania is the canonical bubble with a consolation prize: investors were ruined, but the track remained — the argument now recycled for fibre and AI datacentres.
nuance: “投资狂潮”即英语经济史专名 railway mania——泡沫史的原型案例（Economist 创刊于1843年，正为报道它）。英式 railway / 美式 railroad。“火箭号”= the Rocket。
etymology: rail 源自拉丁 regula（直尺）——铁轨即“铁尺”；way 古英语 weg（路）。

synonyms.sentence: The locomotive shrank the map and standardised the clocks.
A railway mania laid track and bankrupted widows.

zh: 亚当·斯密与“看不见的手”
en: Adam Smith and the invisible hand
def_zh: 英国经济学家（1723–1790），古典政治经济学奠基人：认为经济发展需要自由环境，政府不应干涉经济活动，应留给“看不见的手”（市场）自动调节——“自由放任”理论，成为工业革命的指导思想。著作《国富论》（严复译《原富》）被列为影响世界历史的经典。教材指其局限：过分强调市场、忽视政府调节。
def_en: Founder of classical political economy (1723–90), who argued that prosperity needs freedom: government should stand back and let the 'invisible hand' of the market co-ordinate self-interested actions — laissez-faire, the creed of the industrial age. The Wealth of Nations remains economics' founding text; the textbook adds the standard caveat that markets left wholly alone can fail too.

sources.chapter: 第20课 第一次工业革命

example: Every Economist leader on regulation is a conversation with Smith: how far the invisible hand reaches, and where the visible one must take over.
nuance: the invisible hand（看不见的手）与 laissez-faire（法语“任其自为”）是经济英语的基石词。《国富论》全名 An Inquiry into the Nature and Causes of the Wealth of Nations。注意 Smith 亦著《道德情操论》（The Theory of Moral Sentiments）——“斯密问题”是思想史常谈。“古典政治经济学”= classical political economy。
etymology: invisible（不可见）+ hand——斯密全集仅三用此喻，后世举为大旗。

synonyms.sentence: The free market is a permanent election by purchase.
The division of labour makes pins and dependence.

en: the rise of the labour movement
def_zh: 工业革命使资产阶级愈富而工人待遇很差；19世纪三四十年代工人为改善劳动生活条件、提高政治地位积极斗争：法国里昂工人起义、英国宪章运动、德国西里西亚织工起义——工人阶级开始作为独立政治力量登上历史舞台，迫切需要革命理论指导。
def_en: Industrialisation enriched owners and ground workers; in the 1830s–40s labour fought back — the Lyon silk-weavers' risings, Britain's Chartist movement for the vote, the Silesian weavers' revolt — the working class stepping onto the political stage as a force of its own, in search of a theory.

sources.chapter: 第21课 马克思主义的诞生和国际共产主义运动的兴起

example: The Chartists lost every petition and won every demand: within a century almost the whole People's Charter — votes for all men, paid MPs, secret ballots — was law.
nuance: “宪章运动”= Chartism/the Chartist movement（因《人民宪章》the People's Charter 得名，与《大宪章》呼应）。“工会”= trade union（英）/labor union（美）；“罢工”= strike；“集体谈判”= collective bargaining——劳工报道核心词汇群。
etymology: union 源自拉丁 unus（一）——工会即“合而为一”；trade（行业）古英语“路径”。

synonyms.sentence: Collective bargaining is strike energy stored as procedure.
A strike prices labour by its absence.

en: Marx, Engels and the birth of Marxism
def_zh: 马克思（1818–1883，生于德国特里尔）因抨击普鲁士政府流亡巴黎，结识恩格斯（工厂主之子、曼彻斯特工厂职员），二人成为思想事业上的战友；1849年马克思定居伦敦，在大英博物馆撰写《资本论》等著作。马克思、恩格斯批判地继承前人思想精华，形成马克思主义理论——包括马克思主义哲学、政治经济学和科学社会主义三个组成部分。
def_en: Marx (1818–83), hounded from Prussia for his journalism, met Engels — mill-owner's son with first-hand knowledge of Manchester's factories — in Paris; from London's British Museum reading room Marx produced Das Kapital. Their system — philosophy, political economy, scientific socialism — was built, as they saw it, by critically inheriting the best of what came before.

sources.chapter: 第21课 马克思主义的诞生和国际共产主义运动的兴起

collocations: the point is to change it
example: Marx's tombstone carries the eleventh thesis — 'philosophers have only interpreted the world; the point is to change it' — the most quoted job description in the history of ideas.
nuance: 《资本论》通用德语名 Das Kapital（英译 Capital）。术语体系：历史唯物主义 = historical materialism；科学社会主义 = scientific socialism；剩余价值 = surplus value。英语学界形容词有别：Marxist（政治立场的）与 Marxian（分析方法的，如 Marxian economics）——后者可去政治化使用。“全世界无产者联合起来”= Workers of the world, unite!
etymology: material 源自拉丁 materia（母质，mater 母）——唯物论词根竟是“母亲”。

synonyms.sentence: Class struggle is history's engine, said the exiles in the library.
Surplus value is the unpaid hour audited.

def_zh: 1848年马克思、恩格斯为共产主义者同盟起草的纲领，在伦敦出版：指出有文字记载的全部历史都是阶级斗争的历史；资本主义时代社会日益分裂为资产阶级与无产阶级两大对立阶级，资本主义必将被没有阶级剥削压迫的共产主义取代；亦肯定资产阶级曾起“非常革命的作用”、创造了超过以往一切世代总和的生产力。号召工人阶级组织起来建立自己的政党，以暴力推翻资产阶级统治。其发表标志马克思主义诞生。
def_en: Marx and Engels's 1848 programme for the Communist League: all recorded history is the history of class struggle; capitalism has split society into bourgeoisie and proletariat and will give way to a classless communism — though the bourgeoisie, it concedes, 'has created more massive productive forces than all preceding generations together'. It summons workers to organise as a party and overthrow their rulers; its publication marks Marxism's birth.

sources.chapter: 第21课 马克思主义的诞生和国际共产主义运动的兴起

collocations: nothing to lose but their chains
example: Even its critics quote it for style: 'all that is solid melts into air' has become the stock description of globalisation, coined by its most famous enemy.
nuance: 名句原文值得记诵：A spectre is haunting Europe…（开篇）；The proletarians have nothing to lose but their chains（结尾）。“无产阶级”= the proletariat（源自罗马 proletarii 无产者——与第5课罗马史暗通）；“资产阶级”= the bourgeoisie（承接第9课 burghers 词族）。
etymology: manifesto 意大利语“公开声明”←拉丁 manifestus（显明）——宣言即“摊开手掌”。

synonyms.sentence: The spectre haunts wherever it is banned.
Proletarians of all countries — the addressee line of an era.

en: the First International and the Paris Commune
def_zh: 1864年英法德等国工人代表成立国际工人协会（“第一国际”），马克思为创始人之一，在各国建支部，以提高工资、缩短工时、改善劳动条件为目标进行经济斗争。1870年法国败于普法战争、矛盾激化；1871年3月巴黎无产阶级起义，建立世界上第一个无产阶级政权——巴黎公社（多位领导人为第一国际成员）；5月28日政府军勾结普军反扑，公社失败。《国际歌》诞生于公社背景。
def_en: The International Working Men's Association (1864) — Marx among its founders — organised workers across borders for wages, hours and conditions. After France's defeat by Prussia, the Paris rising of March 1871 created the Commune, the first workers' government in history; it was drowned in blood within ten weeks — and mourned in the Internationale, the anthem written by one of its survivors.

sources.chapter: 第21课 马克思主义的诞生和国际共产主义运动的兴起

example: The Commune's ten weeks have powered a century and a half of argument: to admirers the first workers' democracy, to critics the first proof that revolutionary Paris and governing Paris are different cities.
nuance: “国际工人协会”= the International Working Men's Association；《国际歌》= the Internationale（法语名直接入英语）。commune 词义谱系：中世纪自治城市公社（第9课）→ 巴黎公社 → 人民公社（the people's communes）——同词异史，跨语阅读须辨。镇压周史称 Bloody Week（la Semaine sanglante）。
etymology: inter-（之间）+ nation——“国际”一词本身即19世纪新造。

synonyms.sentence: The commune governed ten weeks and haunts ever since.
A workers' association across borders frightened every border.
